Update 2026 04 25:
It will be 3 years this November since they released part 1 of Spirits of Amazonia. With their 2026 road map saying nothing about finishing the game, I'm finally dropping this to 1 star. I've never seen a developer choose to not finish a game. It's just ridiculous!

Update 2025 08 30:
A couple of years ago, I was thrilled when I finally conquered this game. Then they released the first part of the prequel Spirits of Amazonia (2023 11 30), and that was phenomenal, but then they gave up on the story altogether. Now, they just release multilayer updates. Based on their inability to finish the story mode, I'm lowering my review score. An eternally incomplete game is mostly not worth buying. 

If the abandoned campaign is irrelevant to you then here is the original review.  

Original Review 2023 to 2024: 
I found the release version on Quest 2 in 2022 nigh impossible. Mostly because fats were too scarce, and I kept starving despite the rest of my nutritional intake being decent. After many attempts, I gave up.

I realized in 2023 that they updated, and now fats are more available. I even found a Brazil Nut!!! In all previous attempts, I had never seen one. I'm also now getting fats from coconuts when they originally only have carbs. I have found the Tapir to be the number one best source, and if I can keep Tapir Steaks on hand, then I know I'll be ok.

The point there is if you tried it before, try it again now. It's survivable now. 

Although I've still encountered a couple of glitches such as items falling through the world, and a vanishing machete (probably fell through the world too). The gas can wouldn't open at first. Oh, and there was a spelling error in a story bit. For Incuvo, it was the word "description" in the paper on the wheel chair in the hospital room. I think it says decription. 

There are in-game Save Points now! What a fantastic logical idea that should have been there from the get-go. Even better would be a normal save anywhere system since this is, after all, just a video game and real-life things come up at inconvenient game times!

I would prefer to learn about the in-game world, but all the detailed wiki info I find online pertains to the console version, and a lot of that just doesn't apply here. It would be nice if Incuvo Games could put up a survival guide with real info on what things do and how to make/use them for those of us who prefer information.

It is fun, and playable now, but expect some death while learning the ropes. Also, don't pet the giant kitties!!! 🤚🏻😺

This is a potentially nice game but it's persistent issues with private co-op game play is incredibly frustrating. Me and a friend can not play the game for more than a few days before she can no longer connect to my session. The only way around thus is to start from scratch. Very disappointing.

developer_response.body: Hello, could you reach out to us over at support@incuvo.com, please? We'd love to get more details from you about this issue, so we could investigate what exactly causes it.

reviews.review_description: This game feels like a cheap ripoff of the real game on PCVR and PC. It has almost none of the features as the real game, and it feels like a tiny demo. It gets boring within a few minutes and the functionality and survival is so limited that it just isn't worth it. They are forcing you to pay the same price for something that is a complete scam.

So I have been an avid player of this game since the day I discovered it. I have been waiting patiently for this update and changing your shirt color is what y'all gave us.  First off, we already have paid for the game and the release of the update literally wants you to pay more, to change your clothes. There is no diversity, cannot change your skin color or gender. Just a few colored shirts and shorts. Another thing...Jake walks around the forest cussing all day, yet you make an update that CLEARLY appeals to children.. these outfits look like a Nickelodeon kids special. But to have us waiting so patiently, just to say, here, pay us more money so you can have 3 outfit changes (something that should have been included with base game) is ridiculous. Are y'all gonna make us pay for the new blueprints too? Spring this was supposed to drop..we are in the middle of summer if no one has noticed. I am SUPER DISAPPOINTED, in one of my all time favorite games. Y'all could have done better. Now, will we have to wait 3 more months for the next phase of what was supposed to be a "spring" update?

I thought Dungeons of Eternity was the only VR game worth playing, and then I discovered Green Hell VR. Only reason it gets 3 🌟 is because there isn't an option to allow ONLY the host of a lobby to demolish or deconstruct built items in your lobby that you've built. This would stop the one BIG problem I've encountered is trolls who go room to room destroying or stealing your items. I know there's a private lobby option, but still would be an amazing idea.

It’s is a great game but however, the devs are technically working on it but only adding stuff to make them money, and not actually improving the game’s mechanics or game style instead, the add PLAY CUSTOMIZATION and other stuff to make money instead of adding new animals or new crafting thing in conclusion these devs are the biggest bums of the world

Played it with friends and family made cool base and got animals wanted to become the next drug lords of the Amazon but sadly no drugs or smoking would love to be able to find abandoned cartel drug houses to unlock cigarettes and pipes and to find guns with ammo you find at certain places in the map and for the map to  be larger and to make it look better

Overall rating-9/10
Reason- there are some bugs and graphics could be better as the graphics in Green Hell are great, the game is definetly worth it tho

Green Hell VR is a great and very immersive survival experience. The jungle atmosphere, sounds, and overall environment are extremely well done and can truly pull the player into  the game.

However, there are some details where the realism feels inconsistent. For example, hitting a green coconut twice instantly turns it brown🥥, and it’s possible to drink from it without properly opening it first. Small mechanics like this can break immersion, especially in VR where players notice details much more.🌴

Another issue is the ground and vegetation☘️. In many places, the terrain looks like a flat texture with painted grass instead of real 3D elements. Adding more 3D vegetation would make the world feel much more alive and believable.

One idea that could take the game even further would be adding new environments, such as beaches, coastal areas, or even mountainous regions with climbing mechanics. This would bring more variety and new gameplay possibilities🏝️🏔️.
At the same time, I understand that creating new locations like this would require a lot of work, and it may not be something realistic to expect anytime soon. Still, even smaller improvements in this direction would be very exciting🙂.

Will you add some of my ideas?😊

Thank you And sign!♥️
This is the best survival game on vr, hands down! Learning to live in the jungle is a challenge, and I wish you unlocked crafting recipes through the story, not by discovery, but it's still good 😊

reviews.review_description:
First of let me say that green hell vr is a fun and very imersive game that is fun wether u play solo or multilayer however as much as I love this game I am so tired of being so limited to what I can do.

My usual play through goes like this, either multilayer or survival make an axe and improve it make a stone spear find a place to make camp if im not by the airfield for the machete. Make a fire kill some animals for food get some coconuts for a flask and bowls make a bed a shelter and if I got some time or am near it a mud mixer. After that make a base with a smoker/dryer some planters a couple farmland plots and make a mud brick fire wall. Then keep my food up explore for a bit kill some tribe members get some obsidian get bored and quit.  

I understand that standalone head sets can't handle the same load as pcvr however it would be so much more rewarding and fun if I could make amour or even check my legs for leaches and bits instead of just my arms. Then be able to add a second story to my base or even make a log or stick or rock pile so there not scattered round camp even if I try to stock pile them another thing maybe a chest or a weapon locker maybe even a crafting bench or shelf so that building a bigger shelter dosent feel like a waste of time and resources other than just looking cool. 

Maybe even a crossbow to find or a water bladder added to ur back so i dont have to make 3 canteens to explore (if im gone from base for a while) or even a fishing rod u could craft so I can sit on the bank leach free or a log/bamboo log sledge like the forest or something along those lines just so I can carry more than 2 logs at a time or maybe alow us to make better axes and spears so they dont break as often or a way to fix them like a tool sharpener. Plus just some way to get rid of dead logs and tree stumps to make base desighn even better.

Plus maybe some decorations to spruce up camp like a hanging dream catcher or a animal skull on a stick or mounted would be nice anything along those lines.

This game would be an absolutely 5/5 if it wasn't for the fact myself and others  get bored so quickly iv even had a save that went on for 50+ days that is filled with random stuff and structures just trying to not get bored. Please add some new content maybe even some that is already available in other platforms because some stuff feels so easy to put onto the quest version. 

But at the end of the day it is fun and rewarding but I just can't give it anything higher than a 3 until some updates happen and I know the devs are working very hard plus making a game and updating it is harder than alot of people think but even the little things can add so much depth and i have seen the roadmap and it does look promising and I can't wait to see it but please consider some of these ideas as I think they would add more depth and more hours of fun and even more immersion this is still the best survival game I played and will continue to play even more so if more content gets added and thankyou to the developers for making one of the only amazing games on the quest atm

developer_response.body: Hi! Thanks for the detailed feedback! We're working on updates and more content all the time. Hopefully you've seen the roadmap in the news section we posted not so long ago! We've still got room for the winter content, so make sure to keep following our communication, we hope we won't disappoint you!

​This game is poorly optimized for Quest 3. The frame rate frequently dips into the 50s, which is unacceptable for a smooth VR experience. To ensure stable performance, the developers must simplify the graphic quality or, at the very least, provide adjustable graphical settings.

​Honestly, complaining about "bad graphics" on a standalone headset is remarkably ignorant. People don't seem to understand the hardware limitations of the Quest 3. If you're obsessed with high-fidelity visuals, go play PC VR. Stability should always come before eye candy on standalone gear.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review, with each update we opt for enhancing the experience, so stay tuned, while we try our best!

reviews.review_title: This game sucks and has ruined my life!!
reviews.review_description: Even on high-end hardware, the textures look like they were smeared with Vaseline. It’s hard to feel "immersed" in a lush rainforest when the trees look like green blobs from ten feet away. AlsoVR should make interactions feel natural. Instead, I’m fighting the physics engine just to pick up a stick. The backpack management is a nightmare; I spent more time wrestling with my virtual inventory than actually playing the game. Another reasonTrying to aim a bow or swing a spear feels floaty and unresponsive. Taking down a predator isn't satisfying—it's just a frantic, glitchy arm-flailing session. Overall this game is horrible would not recomend to anyone and i hope it gets taken off the app store. This game has ruined my life and taken so many hours away that i cant have back. And to the developers i hope you are hapoy with what this game has done.

developer_response.body: Hey there, sorry to hear the experience felt this way for you. We’re continuously working on improving visuals, interactions, and overall stability to make the jungle feel more immersive and responsive with each update. While the standalone headsets do have their limits, we are always trying to push them as far as possible. Hopefully, our future updates will change your mind about the game, and all the time put into it won't feel like a waste for you.

Getting killed happens easily, and when you respawn, it’s hard to find your way back to where you were, even with the map. That can get frustrating fast. On top of that, the story can be confusing at times, which makes it harder to stay fully engaged. Overall, it’s an interesting experience, but it can feel difficult and a bit confusing to play.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the thoughtful feedback!
Survival can feel tough at times, that’s part of the experience! However, we’re always looking at ways to improve, so thanks for writing down your thoughts, and stay tuned for the future updates!

reviews.review_title: Best vr game Ive played in years
reviews.review_description: Ever since I saw someone play the forest. I've always wanted to play it. But since I didnt have steam, I couldn't, but recently this year, I had discovered green hell. It was the beat thing I had ever experienced. I hope this game gets better!

I keep seeing videos of people playing Green Hell VR. But they have a different version or game than I have. For example, the backpack in videos it is a grid and it is layed out different. When I play VR I play on meta quest 3 and the backpack has circles. Another example, I saw someone click a tab in their journal that I don't have in my game. The tab had things like a crafting table. In my game I don't have a crafting table. My last example, the axe looks different in everyone else's game. All in all can someone help me understand this because I want to play whatever version everyone else has because it looks way cooler and unique.

developer_response.body:
Hi there, sounds like you might be referring to Green Hell VR on PCVR. A gaming PC can handle far more complex systems, larger maps, and higher visual fidelity, while standalone VR has to run everything directly on the headset, essentially like running a full game on mobile hardware.

Because of that, some features can’t always be brought over 1:1 from PC, but the team keeps working to push those limits with every update and expand what’s possible on Quest.

reviews.review_description: Is there armor in green hell cause I haven’t played this game for like a year.

Cool concept that it's on standalone with multiplayer, but Please make it like the PC vr version with all the extra things such as builds, items, mobs, and features. Plus, the multi-player on the SteamVR version would be sick.🙏🙏

developer_response.body:
Hi there! Standalone Quest headsets have much tighter hardware limits than PCs, everything runs directly on the headset, so some features from PCVR can’t be brought over 1:1. That said, we’re always pushing those limits with every update.

Good news though: with the 2026 roadmap, we’re bringing the PSVR2 version to PCVR as an option, which means you’ll be able to play multiplayer there.

reviews.review_description:
Chaotic survival/building/resource game where items don't last forever (foods spoil) and when your teammate dies or you eat a bug you start to have auditory and visual hallucinations. 

The hallucinated resources like coconuts are hilarious as heck or frustrating as hell depending on whether your character is dying of thirst haha
I like this game it is so fun if you’re playing multiplayer and I like how you have to actually make your stuff

Great survival game that I play a lot in co-op with a friend. Are there any upcoming updates? We’ve had some bugs, like saved games that we can’t load in co-op anymore. Will there also be new construction updates to make the VR version more complete?
I still definitely recommend it because it’s an amazing game!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! Yes, the team is working on updates, so stay tuned! :)

reviews.review_description: I absolutely love every aspect of this game , from the building mechanics to the awesome visuals to the intense survival aspect end up big predators that can jump out at any time

I would ask for a bit more tho 
More fruit I think would be cool 
Definitely more fish
Monkeys we need monkeys 
Bats in caves
6 player lobbies 
More weapons
And infection or parasite option for custom 



Okay the big four I would really want

TIGERS! Just like the Jaguar but bigger and more health you can also use some of its skin for warmth

GREEN ANACONDAS would be a great inclusion their just like the rattlesnakes but not venomous and are silent I think it would be cool if they lurked in the canopy area

SHARKS most dangerous in deep water basically their bull sharks and are just large piranhas 

And finally   HIPPOS 🦛 would be outstanding basically impossible to kill they lurk by deep areas and are very vicious towards humans 

So thanks for a really realistic fun game you should definitely get it it’s so fun and scary

reviews.review_title: By far the best VR game.
reviews.review_description: It's an incredible game, well-designed and immersive, where crafting is dynamic and every detail must be taken seriously. An example of this is how a simple cut can quickly lead to your death. Great game! 🌟

This game is great with graphics and building is a little bugged but it’s still playable but I have some suggestions they should a mods tab to make the game for versatile and make it more challenging and also adding new animals like monkeys or hippos and making the new players in multiplayer have destroying stuff disabled because griefing is something I deal with everyday now but currently still an amazing game

Edit: please add fishing and chests please devs

reviews.review_title: One of the best games.. EVER.
reviews.review_description:
Honestly.. this is one of the BEST vr games ive EVER played.. in this game you need to survive in the amazon jungle from stuff like tribesman to legit scorpions.. every moment, feels like your there.. every insect.. you can hear.. anything can and will kill you.. even your own body.. drink river water? You get parasites..  walk too far from base? Your gonna get lacerations on your arms from a puma sneaking up on you..acidentally walk past a spider? You get venom flowing through your blood.. dont sleep or sleep to little.. you go insane and get insomia.. eat expired/spoiled meat? You get parasites.. i know this all sounds difficult but this is why it's called "GREEN HELL".. little advice from me: imagine your actually there.. you will instinctivly hear animals, sense pumas, and see prey before they see you.. the animals are stronger.. but you adapt, you make weapons.. carve spears and hunt more skilfully.. forgot to mention you have a carbs,protein,nutrients and hydration bar.. if one goes to 0? You get tired.. and start to see things that arent there since you will go insane.. or.. are they?
Anyways.. actually recommend

As someone who owns the screen is vr version the vr version does everything much better.  On the vr version the map is smaller and I understand why it's small but on the screen version map os bigger also on the vr version you can see yourself on the map but on the screen version you can't.  But I get why you can't see yourself on the map on the screen version since it's a survival game but I would love where they would tell you where you sre on the screen versions map.
I had so much fun playing this game and I don’t know what to say about it

reviews.review_title: Unable to play since the quest two was
reviews.review_description: So now I can’t play with my friend. He has a quest three. I have a Quest2. My quest automatically updated last night. Ever since then I can join his game BUT he can not see me and I can not see him I just just all black, nothing around please fix it so that this was not a waste of money. I have a video and a screenshot to show. Very disappointing.￼

developer_response.body: Hi! This is indeed an odd issue, and we would really appreciate your help here. Could you reach out to us at support@incuvo.com with the video and screenshot? We'll also guide you on how to send us your save file, which might help us locate and fix this problem.

reviews.review_title: 5 star game held hostage by bugs
reviews.review_description:
The game is amazing — genuinely my current obsession — but oh boy, the glitches are out here living their best chaotic lives. Items vanish the moment I drop them, like they’ve decided to torture me. Trees and plants refuse to produce anything, as if they’ve unionized and gone on strike. Animals drop zero meat, which is honestly rude. And trying to join a game only to be yeeted into a black abyss? That’s a level of existential dread I did not sign up for.  Is this an Oculus thing?  
I want to give this masterpiece a 5‑star rating, I really do, but the bugs are working overtime. Fix them, and I’ll happily throw all five stars at you like confetti

developer_response.body: Hey there, thanks for reaching out to us and taking the time to write a review. We'll look into the issues you've described as we continue working on the game! We would love to get a save file from you, so our devs could investigate all this even faster. So, please reach out to us over at support@incuvo.com, and we'll guide you through obtaining a save file :) Thank you!

reviews.review_description:
I'm not a fan of survival games. However, what convinced me to buy this game was the chance to play with friends, and... I'm really impressed - the entire story mode can be played in co-op!!! Not many games can offer that.

Interacting with the world is really interesting, the game’s progression, learning new mechanics, and building increasingly advanced gear and structures is very satisfying.

For a standalone game, and given the sheer amount of content it offers, it runs surprisingly smoothly.

Played the game survival mode went to the over turned jeep and surprise the grapple hook refuses to spawn in I tried restarting the game I even made a shelter to save by the jeep nothing works all that time and effort for nothing devs fix this it's literally unplayable when this happens this happens right after y'all release a path saying you fixed this it's a rip off and money grabber 
Edit : the iron pot didn't spawn either

developer_response.body: Hi, could you reach out to us at support@incuvo.com, please? We would need your save file to investigate what happened. We'll gladly guide you over emails on how to do it :) Cheers!
